first.speaker: The Keeper
first.text: You found the shrine. Most find the light first, and the sweeping never. I do the sweeping.

choices.text: There's one who walks the valley. You know them?

again.speaker: The Keeper
again.text: The dust returns. So do you.

choices.text: There's one who walks the valley. You know them?
The Wanderer says the west path cleared.

tend.speaker: The Keeper
tend.text: I keep the platform clear and the quiet unbroken. The light does the rest, whatever the rest is.

light.speaker: The Keeper
light.text: It was here before the path was. It will be here after the path forgets us. I sweep; it shines. We don't discuss it.

knows_wanderer.speaker: The Keeper
knows_wanderer.text: Since before either of us kept count. They walk; I stay. Between the two of us, the valley is held.

message.speaker: The Keeper
message.text: …Then the stones can rest a season. Thank them — if the wind lets you meet twice.
